description: Declares that EVERY M1 flow ships state=off (draft / not-activated) inside the CollisionSpike solution. Activation is the user's reserved step. authoringBoundary tags the offline build of the definition ([BUILD]); activationBoundary tags who turns it on. The linter asserts every definition file is listed here with state=off.
globalRule: No flow ships activated. Importing the solution lands every flow draft/off; the user activates after binding connections + confirming DLP. Claude never activates a flow, never points a trigger at a live mailbox, never runs against live SharePoint/Box/EVA.

flows.reservedReason: Trigger points at a live Collision Engineers shared inbox. Activation + all live-inbox tests are reserved.

goLiveHardening.minIntakeDateGuard: First action after the trigger (Drop_if_before_min_date) drops any email whose receivedDateTime is before the MinIntakeDate flow parameter (String, default '2026-06-17', set per-environment at activation) via Terminate runStatus=Succeeded, so historical backlog mail is never ingested. Audits dropped_before_min_date reusing the existing duplicate_dropped action value 100000005 (no Dataverse schema change).
goLiveHardening.fetchOnlyWithAttachment: PHASE-8 (ADR-0015): FLIPPED true->false offline — the email-management/routing system has landed (triage-first restructure: Find_existing_inbound dedup + Run_triage child (CS_TriageClassify) + Switch on category). The old attachment-only filter was a documented TEMPORARY noise filter to remove once routing landed. Flipping the LIVE trigger true->false + the single-inbox soft rollout (G6) and reconciling Run_case_resolve/Run_enrich up to live before re-import (G1) remain operator-gated; see docs/plans/phase-8-inbox-management/intake-restructure-notes.md. state stays off.

flows.reservedReason: Parameterised per-inbox multi-mailbox intake. Trigger points at a live Collision Engineers inbox (shared or licensed). Activation, the Exchange Full-Access grant / new-connection sign-in, the designer webhook publish, and all live-inbox tests are reserved. See docs/activation/multi-inbox-activation.md.

goLiveHardening.minIntakeDateGuard: First action after the trigger (Drop_if_before_min_date) drops any email whose receivedDateTime is before the MinIntakeDate flow parameter (String, default '2026-06-17', set per-inbox at activation = that inbox's go-live date) via Terminate runStatus=Succeeded, so a freshly-connected inbox never ingests its historical backlog. Audits dropped_before_min_date reusing the existing duplicate_dropped action value 100000005 (no Dataverse schema change).
goLiveHardening.hasAttachments: PHASE-8 (ADR-0015): FLIPPED true->false offline (parallel to intake.definition.json) — triage-first restructure (Find_existing_inbound dedup + Run_triage child + Switch on category). This thinner per-inbox variant has no downstream child chain and is not live, so there is no live wiring to reconcile here. Flipping the LIVE trigger + single-inbox soft rollout remain operator-gated (G6). state stays off.
goLiveHardening.perInboxParameterisation: IntakeMailbox is the ONLY per-copy difference: one flow per inbox, identical JSON, different IntakeMailbox value bound to the V2 trigger's mailboxAddress and stamped on each Case as cr1bd_sourcemailbox. SHARED-mailbox inbox -> reuse the existing cr1bd_sharedmailbox_office365 (digital@) connection (no new password). LICENSED-user inbox -> bind that same reference to a NEW Office 365 connection signed in as the user, OR grant digital@ Full Access and treat as shared.

flows.reservedReason: Processes real inbound mail bytes/attachments; runs only against live mail when reserved.

flows.activationNote: Repurposed from the orphaned ADR-0010 dedup ladder to MERGE-BY-REGISTRATION: a single complementary instructions<->images pair on the same registration auto-merges into the instructions case (the one carrying the Case/PO), which re-runs status-evaluate and lands in Review when complete; more than one complementary candidate -> duplicate_risk/Held for staff (one reg can carry two legitimate claims, ADR-0010); zero -> no-op. Request-triggered child (no Office-365 webhook) so safe to edit + recompile (deactivate->reactivate). Turned ON live 2026-06-20 under the live-services override; the merge fires only on a genuine single complementary pair (none exist in the data today) and a human reviews every merged case, so live risk is low. Called by CS Intake via Run_case_resolve after Run_parse, non-blocking, with { caseId } — VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-21 (live CS Intake invokes classify-persist/parse/status-evaluate/enrich/case-resolve). The repo intake.definition.json trails live (the call is not yet in the repo def) — reconcile before a solution re-import; live is authoritative.
flows.trigger: Request (called by intake via Run_case_resolve after parse — LIVE, verified 2026-06-21; repo intake def trails live)

flows.reservedReason: Invoked across the live pipeline; ships off until the pipeline is activated.
Calls the parser Function on real instruction bytes; parser Function deploy is [DEPLOY-WITH-LOGIN], flow activation reserved.
Calls the DVSA enrichment Function via the gateway on real VRMs; gateway secret inject is [RESERVED-FOR-USER].
Part of the live intake chain; matches real sender domains and writes provider binding.
Running against the LIVE SharePoint job sheet is reserved; [BUILD] verification uses a synthetic/redacted fixture only.
EVA submit + Box sync against live EVA/Box; activation + live test + EVA prod cutover are reserved.
Draft-only by design (no send action exists); activation of any outbound path is reserved and lives in a separate kill-switched flow.

Sends REAL outbound email across the live-services boundary. Behind the CHASER_SEND_ENABLED kill switch (default false); whatsapp-channel chasers are hard-skipped (manual send only, ADR-0003). Flipping the gate + turning this flow ON is the operator's reserved step; Claude never enables it.

Phase-7 Box pivot. Mints the UPPERCASE Case/PO Box folder at parse-confirm via the custom Box REST connector (CCG token minted server-side in the box-webhook Function). Importing the cr1bd_box_rest connector + binding its connection (Function host key) + setting cr1bd_BOX_FOLDER_ROOT_ID + flipping BOX_API_ENABLED then BOX_FOLDER_AT_INTAKE_ENABLED are reserved. The intake invocation (inside Scope_generate_casepo after Update_case_casepo) is an operator/business-phase LIVE edit (the repo intake def trails live — see box-integration-activation.md). Claude never holds a Box credential, never binds the connection, never flips the gate.
flows.trigger: Request (child of intake at parse-confirm, and of case-resolve's survivor-folder ensure)

flows.reservedReason: Phase-7 Box pivot (B2). Authored STANDBY child: copies the hand-built TEMPLATE File Request onto a case's Box folder and returns the live uploader URL, ready for future operator activation. NOT currently invoked by the Code App — under CSP connect-src 'none' the Code App calls the cr1bd_box_rest connector op (CopyFileRequest / GetFolderSharedLink) DIRECTLY with no flow in the path (pinned 2026-06-21 build-plan decision). Hand-building the ONE template File Request + recording its id (cr1bd_BOX_FILE_REQUEST_TEMPLATE_ID), importing/binding cr1bd_box_rest, flipping BOX_API_ENABLED then BOX_FILEREQUEST_ENABLED, and the BLOCKING B2 FILE.UPLOADED live-test are reserved. Claude never holds a Box credential, never flips the gate.
flows.trigger: Request (authored standby; the Code App uses the direct cr1bd_box_rest connector op (no flow in the path) — ready for operator activation)

flows.reservedReason: Phase-8 inbox triage (ADR-0015). Request-triggered CHILD called once per inbound email by the generalised intake (which flips fetchOnlyWithAttachment false). Classifies EVERY email via the parser connector's deterministic ClassifyEmail (/classify-email) op, upserts one cr1bd_inboundemail row keyed on cr1bd_sourcemessageid, runs the open-Case lookup (Case/PO first, VRM fallback) and NEVER auto-links on ambiguity (ADR-0010). Importing the /classify-email route on the parser connector, applying the cr1bd_inboundemail table + the two triage choicesets + the inbound_classified/inbound_routed audit actions (26-inbound-email.ps1), the intake restructure (flip the trigger, generalise dedup, insert the triage child + Switch), and the single-inbox soft rollout are reserved. Claude never activates the flow or points the trigger at a live inbox.
flows.trigger: Request (child of intake, once per inbound email)

flows.reservedReason: Phase-7 Box pivot (Wave 5). Scheduled, status-driven Blob cleanup once Box is the archive of record: deletes ONLY archived (accepted-for-EVA, non-excluded) IMAGE Blob evidence bytes for box_synced cases past the PurgeGraceDays window (non-image transient bytes are retained — a deferred follow-up; never the Box copy, never the Evidence row). Setting a real near-term Recurrence startTime (it ships a far-future placeholder so it never fires on deploy), pinning PurgeGraceDays, binding cr1bd_evidenceblob, and flipping BOX_API_ENABLED are reserved. Claude never flips the gate or binds the connection.

Phase-9 data governance (ADR-0017 G1, DEFERRED-pending-operator/legal). THE MOST SENSITIVE flow: scheduled, gate-read AUTOMATED PII handling. Reads the two-clock retention columns (cr1bd_retentionexpiresat / cr1bd_legalhold / cr1bd_closedat) and, ONLY for terminal/closed cases whose GDPR minimisation window has lapsed AND with NO litigation/evidential hold, purges remaining transient Azure Blob bytes (first-party shared_azureblob DeleteFile_V2) + anonymises (field-null, NOT row hard-delete) the claimant PII on the Case + its Evidence, clears cr1bd_retentionexpiresat as the disposition marker, and audits case_disposed (100000026) per case. NEVER deletes from Box (one-way mirror, ADR-0012 §8a) — no Box op anywhere. NEVER deletes cr1bd_auditevent rows (audit-of-record). Behind the destructive CASE_DISPOSITION_ENABLED kill switch (default false; READ never written) — the flow is INERT until the operator BOTH flips it true AND turns the flow ON. Activation requires the [RESERVED-FOR-USER]/legal retention period (drives cr1bd_retentionexpiresat elsewhere), the ADR-0017 G6 store-hardening pre-step (KV purge-protection + Blob soft-delete/versioning), binding shared_azureblob + Dataverse, and setting a real near-term Recurrence startTime (ships a far-future placeholder so it never fires on import). The anonymise-vs-hard-delete CHOICE is operator policy — the flow defaults to the conservative field-null; the hard-delete variant is a clearly-marked gated-off placeholder comment, NOT implemented. Claude never flips the gate, never binds the connection, never turns the flow on.

summary.activationNote: All flows ship off EXCEPT case-resolve (merge-by-registration), turned ON live 2026-06-20 under the live-services override (Claude wires activations directly). Every other flow's activation remains the operator's reserved step. Phase-7 added three Box flows (box-folder-create, box-file-request-copy, box-blob-purge), all state=off, gated by BOX_* env-vars (default OFF); case-resolve gained an idempotent survivor-folder ensure (still ON). Phase-8 added the triage-classify child (state=off, ADR-0015), called once per inbound email to classify into the cr1bd_inboundemail triage table. Phase-9 added the case-disposition scheduled flow (state=off, ADR-0017 G1, gated by the default-OFF destructive CASE_DISPOSITION_ENABLED kill switch) — the two-clock retention disposition (anonymise + transient-Blob purge; never Box, never audit rows).
